issues.title: Euro symbol appears on Quick Accent after the update 100
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the screenshot. It shows € (U+20AC) appearing in Quick Accent. Could you confirm your selected character set/language in Quick Accent settings and whether this started only after updating to 0.100?

Could you add your monitor topology (built-in/external count, scaling, and which display is primary) plus whether the dock moves after sleep/resume, unplug/replug, or only when switching project modes?

issues.title: Improve the UI/UX of Awake function
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the suggestion. The screenshot shows Awake preserving the previous timed interval (3h 1m). Can you clarify the preferred UX: should enabling Awake always reset to a default mode, prompt for a new interval, or remember the previous interval but make it more obvious?

issues.title: Command palette dock is unstable with frequent Crashes. On multiple screen, the docks for different screens get moved around from one screen to the other
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for reporting. This looks related to #48516. Could you add your display layout (number of monitors, scaling, primary display), and whether the dock moves after sleep/resume, unplugging a display, or opening Command Palette?

issues.title: dmidecode reports No SMBIOS nor DMI entry point found (/dev/mem exists, missing 000F0000 range)
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the additional use case. The new note that sysfs DMI support is required by apps such as Sober, and appears related to #13958, is useful context for prioritization. Maintainers: consider cross-linking #13958 here and clarifying whether exposing SMBIOS/DMI in WSL2 is in scope.

issues.title: Cannot enable Mirrored network on WSL
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the updated screenshot. It confirms this still reproduces on WSL 2.7.10 with CreateVm/ConfigureNetworking/0x803b0015, plus the message that the current request is unsupported and that localhost proxy is not mirrored into WSL NAT mode. If anyone else can still reproduce, please add Windows build, WSL version, .wslconfig networking section, VPN/firewall software, and wsl --debug-shell/networking logs.

issues.title: fix: suppress getaddrinfo EAI_NODATA error
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for sticking with this. The PR has reviewer attention and requested maintainers, but it still appears blocked. Could a maintainer summarize the remaining required change or validation needed before merge, especially around the new enum/measure update mentioned earlier?

issues.title: [Bug] WSL2 GPU passthrough has 16 GiB CUDA driver overhead on Blackwell sm_120 — blocks PyTorch large allocations
issues.suggested_reply:
Thanks @nakaken3013-code — the 	orch.cuda.init()-only repro is a useful narrowing point because it removes vLLM/SGLang/NCCL and expandable-segments from the equation. Could you add the exact Windows build, WSL version, NVIDIA driver version, and 
vidia-smi output for that 2x RTX 5070 setup? That should help compare it with the original RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell report.

[WSL 2.9.3] Docker fails to bind ports with "address already in use" when networkingMode=mirrored is enabled
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks @Guest-Liang — PostgreSQL on 5433 and Redis on 6379 failing directly inside WSL is an important data point because it shows this is not limited to Docker port publishing. Please add the exact error text from each service and whether disabling mirrored networking or downgrading from WSL 2.9.3 immediately restores binding.

issues.title: Start the plan9 server on WSL2 even when automount and mountFsTab are…
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the focused fix and for linking it to #41056. Since this touches src/linux/init/config.cpp and changes WSL1-vs-WSL2 Plan9 startup behavior, maintainers should review the WSL1 guard carefully. @microsoft/wslc-team is already requested via CODEOWNERS; no individual reviewer evidence found.

issues.title: WSL2/WSLg: dxgkrnl memcpy field-spanning write + ENOBUFS telemetry; DxgKrnl-SysMm provider invalid, Lxss provider missing
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for filing this. It appears to overlap heavily with #41060, which includes additional triage detail. Please attach the requested WSL logs here or confirm we should continue in #41060 so maintainers can track one canonical report.

issues.title: WSL2/WSLg: dxgkrnl memcpy field-spanning write + ENOBUFS telemetry; DxgKrnl-SysMm provider invalid, Lxss provider missing
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the detailed logs and triage summary. This looks actionable: dxgkrnl reports a FORTIFY field-spanning write in dxgvmb_send_wait_sync_object_gpu, with ENOBUFS telemetry and host Hyper-V symptoms. Could you confirm whether #41059 is the same report and close/merge one of the two to keep investigation in one place? Maintainers: this likely belongs with the WSLg/GPU path plus telemetry diagnostics.

issues.title: Localization change from build: 151997771
WSLg 1.0.71: GUI session freezes, then Weston becomes unkillable in /mnt/shared_memory virtiofs
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the very detailed diagnostics. The D-state stacks around equest_wait_answer on /mnt/shared_memory and irtio_fs_drain_queue make this look worth investigating as a WSLg shared-memory/virtiofs hang rather than an app deadlock. If you can reproduce or catch it again, could you add whether it also occurs with the mirrored-networking/autoMemoryReclaim settings disabled, and whether any smaller GUI workload can trigger it?
